div.head: The Minister in China (Johnson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: My December 19, 9 a.m. I have just had a talk with C. C. Wu who tells me that the Government is going to be very much preoccupied with governmental reorganization during next 2 weeks. He said that posts of Foreign Affairs and Finance in new Cabinet were extremely uncertain. He himself expects to take Chairmanship of Kwangtung.
I told him, as I told Koo yesterday, that I was going north on MondayDecember 21, 1931. to return shortly after the new year.
I and my French colleagues are expecting to dine informally with General Chiang Kai-shek Sunday evening. My British colleague leaves Shanghai today by Japanese merchant vessel for Tientsin.
